text.heading: Committee members disagree over Adedibu’s funeral
text.p: From Oseheye Okwuofu, and Bisi Oladele,Ibadan

Disagreement among members of the funeral committee for prominent politician and Ashipa Olubadan of Ibadan, Chief Lamidi Ariyibi Adedibu, may mar the eight-day fidau = Islamic ritual of prayers or ceremonies held in honour of deceased members, and which can last several days (ew) prayer for the deceased, scheduled for tomorrow.
The committee’s meeting on the funeral programme ended in a deadlock yesterday as members engaged in altercations.
The disagreement, it was gathered, was between the late Adedibu’s Personal Assistant, Alhaji Hazeem Gbolarumi, and some members of the committee.

Besides Gbolarumi, other members of the committee, headed by the Deputy Governor, Hon. Taofeek Arapaja, include Secretary to the State Government (SSG), Chief Olayiwola Olakojo, Dr. Saka Balogun, Hon. Folake Olunloyo- Oshinowo and Senator Kamorudeen Adedibu.
Gbolarumi however denied the allegation, saying "we only disagreed to agree and we are working towards a common goal."
It was gathered that the 15-member committee had been divided into sub-committees.
The committee’s recommendations are subject to the approval of Governor Adebayo Alao-Akala who is being expected back home from a trip to Paris with President Umar Musa Yar’Adua.
Gbolarumi, in a telephone interview, confirmed that the committee members disagreed on the funeral programme.

He said: "We did not fight. But you cannot rule out disagreement on things like these. What happened is that some people wanted some things to go up; others said no, let them go down. We held our meeting today (yesterday) to finalise the whole programme; we had to disagree because some were not okay and so, there were some disagreements.
"It is not true at all that we fought at our meeting.
How can that happen? Whatever we agree upon will still be subjected to the approval of the Governor, Otunba Christopher Alao-Akala."
According to him, members of the committee are still waiting for the governor for his final say on the funeral programme.
An Ibadan High Court yesterday joined the executor of the late Adedibu’s will in a suit challenging the destruction of the property of Managing Director, Pelly Foam Limited Mr. Dapo Davies in 2006.
Justice A.L. Akintola granted the prayer of Davies to substitute the late Adedibu’s name with that of the executor of his will.

Davies dragged Mr. Peter Asha, Chairman of King Zion Ventures, one of the principal officers in his company, Sola Oyin-Adejobi, Akeem Adebayo (deceased) and King Zion Venture to court over the destruction of his property, worth N1 billion ,by hoodlums suspected to be loyal to the late Adedibu.
But the hurdle before Davies is to find the name of the executor before July 21, to present it in his application to the court at its next sitting.
Conflicting reports have been emanating from the late politician’s children and associates on whether he left a will or died intestate.
While one of his children, Dauda, claimed that he left no will, others said he did.

At the resumed hearing of the suit yesterday, Davies’ counsel, Mr. E.A Adesuyi, told the court that in view of the death of Adedibu, he would like to amend the case document to reflect the executor of the late Adedibu’s will.
Justice Akintola adjourned suit till July 21 for further hearing.
